conditions.name: Dying
conditions.description: You are bleeding out or otherwise at death's door. Automatically applied when you fall to 0 HP without instant death. While Dying, you are Unconscious. At the start of each of your turns at 0 HP you make a death saving throw (no ability mod, DC 10 on a d20). Three successes = stable; three failures = death. A natural 1 counts as two failures; a natural 20 restores 1 HP and removes two failed saves. Damage at 0 HP imposes one failure (two for a critical, instant death if damage equals or exceeds your HP maximum). Successful saves reset to zero on regaining HP or stabilizing; failures reset only after a short rest.

conditions.name: Incapacitated
conditions.description: An Incapacitated creature can't take actions or reactions, and cannot maintain Concentration or Mold Chakra.

conditions.name: Stunned
conditions.description: Completely paralyzed, unable to move or act. The creature is Incapacitated, can't move, and can speak only falteringly. Automatically fails Strength and Dexterity saves. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage.

conditions.name: Exhaustion
conditions.description: Some abilities and environmental hazards (long-term freezing or scorching, etc.) cause Exhaustion. Each rank applies: -1 to all dice rolls, -1 to AC, -1 to all Save DCs. At 11 ranks the creature dies. A long rest with food and drink reduces Exhaustion by 2; effects that remove Exhaustion remove no more than 1 rank unless otherwise stated.

conditions.name: Unconscious
conditions.description: Total mental remission or asleep. Incapacitated, can't move or speak, unaware of surroundings. Drops what it's holding and falls Prone. Auto-fails all saves. Attack rolls against it have advantage; any hit from within 5 ft is a critical.

conditions.name: Petrified
conditions.description: Transformed (along with anything worn/carried) into a solid inanimate substance, usually stone. Weight increases ten-fold and aging stops. Incapacitated, can't move or speak, unaware of surroundings. Attack rolls against it have advantage. Auto-fails Strength and Dexterity saves. Resistance to all damage. Immune to poison and disease (any already in the system is suspended, not neutralized).

conditions.name: Burned
conditions.description:
Takes 1d8 Fire damage at the start of each of its turns per rank.

Ending early: spend an Action to make a DC 15 Dexterity or Constitution check; on success the affliction ends. Submerging fully in water also ends Burned.

conditions.name: Chilled
conditions.description:
Body and nerves overwhelmed by cold. Once per turn, when the creature willingly moves more than 5 feet, it takes 1d10 cold damage per rank.

Ending early: spend an Action to make a DC 15 Dexterity or Constitution check; on success the affliction ends.

conditions.name: Corroded
conditions.description:
A penalty is applied to the creature's DR (damage reduction) equal to the rank of the jutsu that inflicted it: D-Rank: -2, C-Rank: -4, B-Rank: -6, A-Rank: -8, S-Rank: -10.

Ending early: spend an Action to make a DC 15 Dexterity or Constitution check; on success the affliction ends. Submerging fully in water also ends Corroded.

conditions.name: Shocked
conditions.description: Can never make more than one attack, or cast more than one jutsu, per turn. Loses 1 rank of Shocked at the end of each of its turns unless otherwise specified.

conditions.name: Envenomed
conditions.description: Has been exposed to poison, venom, or toxin, causing vitality to drop rapidly. -1 to AC per rank. Cannot regain hit points and cannot gain the benefits of a rest. Creatures immune to poison damage are immune to Envenomed.

conditions.name: Bruised
conditions.description: Suffered a traumatic blow causing increased vulnerability. Takes +4 bonus damage from all sources. A creature can spend an Action to make a DC 15 Strength check to end the condition.

conditions.name: Encumbered
conditions.description:
• Your Speed is Halved.
• You have disadvantage on all checks, attack rolls and saving throws, that use Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution.

conditions.name: Staggered
conditions.description: Completely overwhelmed by pain or has had its battle senses shaken. Cannot take Disengage, Dash, or Dodge actions. Disadvantage on all Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution attacks, saves, and checks. Ends at the end of the affected creature's next turn.

conditions.name: Bleed
conditions.description: Suffered a minor abrasion causing slow blood loss. Takes 1d6 necrotic damage per rank at the beginning of each of its turns. As an Action a creature can attempt to stop the bleeding with a DC 20 Medicine check, removing one rank; for every +2 above the DC, remove +1 additional rank.

conditions.name: Dazed
conditions.description: Recovering from a powerful physical or mental blow. Can only either move or take an action — not both. Cannot take a bonus action. Lasts until the end of the affected creature's next turn.

conditions.name: Shaken
conditions.description: Physically destabilized from physical stress. -1 penalty to all d20 rolls made using Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution per rank. Loses 1 rank at the end of each of its turns unless otherwise specified.

conditions.name: Grappled
conditions.description: Usually partially restrained — typically by another creature or a trap. Speed becomes 0 and cannot benefit from any speed bonus. Attacks at disadvantage. Ends if the grappler is Incapacitated or if an effect moves the grappled creature out of the grappler's reach or the original grappling space.

conditions.name: Restrained
conditions.description: Completely pinned in place, unable to move from its space. Counts as Grappled (and ends if the Grappled condition ends). Attack rolls against it have advantage. Disadvantage on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saves.

conditions.name: Prone
conditions.description: Down on the ground. Only movement option is to crawl up to half its speed. Disadvantage on attack rolls. Ranged attacks against it have disadvantage; melee attacks against it have advantage. Spending half movement to stand up dispels the condition; if speed is 0, cannot stand.

conditions.name: Weakened
conditions.description: Unable to exert maximum physicality. Reduces damage dealt by -4 per rank.

conditions.name: Concussed
conditions.description: Experienced a traumatic blow causing mental acuity to suffer. -1 penalty to all Save DCs per rank. A creature can spend an Action with a Medicine kit to make a DC 20 Medicine check, ending the condition on a success.

conditions.name: Demoralized
conditions.description: Mentally disturbed with heightened stress. -1 penalty to all d20 rolls made using Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma per rank. Loses 1 rank at the end of each of its turns unless otherwise specified.

conditions.name: Berserk
conditions.description: A Compelled form. The Berserk creature treats the nearest creature to it as its priority target for combat, except its compeller. If multiple creatures are equally in range, it picks one at random. Ends at the end of the affected creature's next turn.

conditions.name: Charmed
conditions.description: A Compelled form. The Charmed creature's Attitude rank with its compeller is increased: D/C-Rank +1, B/A-Rank +2, S-Rank +3. It will not take hostile actions against its compeller. Ends early if the compeller or its allies harm the charmed creature in any way.

conditions.name: Confused
conditions.description: A Compelled form. The Confused creature forgets the most critical parts of jutsu, becoming unable to cast jutsu requiring Hand Seals (HS). Ends at the end of the affected creature's next turn.

conditions.name: Slowed
conditions.description: Feels as though in slow motion, akin to walking in water. Speed is reduced by half and cannot gain bonuses to speed for the duration. Cannot take a Reaction. Ends at the end of the affected creature's next turn unless otherwise specified.

conditions.name: Sealed
conditions.description: Has a limiter on its chakra network — usually the result of a Fuinjutsu. Increases the base chakra cost of Ninjutsu and Genjutsu by +4 per rank. A creature with 3+ ranks cannot teleport by any means. As an action, a creature can make a DC 20 Ninshou check to remove 1 rank; on a failure, the DC increases by +4.

conditions.name: Blinded
conditions.description: Cannot see. Cannot target or select another creature unless they have Blindsight, Tremor Sense, or Chakra Sensing. Cannot benefit from clan features with the [Dojutsu] tag (e.g. Sharingan, Byakugan). Auto-fails any check requiring sight. Disadvantage on Dexterity, Intelligence, and Wisdom saves. Attack rolls against the creature have advantage; its attack rolls have disadvantage (unless using one of the special senses above). A creature can willingly close their eyes as a free action, imposing this condition until the start of their next turn.

conditions.name: Dazzled
conditions.description: Senses overloaded. -2 penalty to all checks and attack rolls per rank. Cannot gain advantage on attack rolls. Reduces the range of attacks and jutsu by half (minimum 5 feet). A creature can make a DC 15 Constitution check as an action to remove all ranks.

conditions.name: Deafened
conditions.description: Cannot hear. Auto-fails any check requiring hearing. -10 penalty to Perception. Creatures hidden from a Deafened creature do not need to reroll Stealth to maintain stealth against them. A creature can willingly cover their ears as a bonus action, imposing this condition (and occupying both hands) until the start of their next turn.

conditions.name: Invisible
conditions.description: Impossible to see without a special sense. For hiding purposes, the creature is heavily obscured; its location can be detected by noise it makes or tracks it leaves. Attack rolls against it have disadvantage and its attack rolls have advantage, unless it can be perceived by a special sense.

conditions.name: Identity Crisis
conditions.description: A creature suffering from an Identity Crisis finds themselves to be their own enemy. Each turn they spend all of their actions attacking themselves with damaging jutsu, effects and abilities, treating each attack as a critical hit. If one of their allies spends their Reaction talking to the creature with an Identity Crisis they can remake their saving throw at advantage.

conditions.name: Mentally Compromised
conditions.description:
A Mentally Compromised creature finds it difficult to shut out psychic intrusion. They treat a failure as a Critical failure vs Genjutsu with the Tactile keyword. The first time this effect occurs, this condition ends.

Additionally, once per turn, when a Mentally Compromised creature makes a Concentration check to maintain concentration on a jutsu, they must first spend 4 Chakra to make the check; if they choose not to, they immediately lose concentration as if they failed the check. This effect increases in strength at 6th level (6 Chakra), 10th (8), 14th (10) and 18th (12) levels.

Enhanced Effect (Lvl 10): A Mentally Compromised creature radiates a 15ft radius aura of madness. The first time each turn a creature allied to it within its madness aura casts a Jutsu, they must roll a DC 11 Flat Check. On a failure, the Genjutsu Specialist can cause the failing creature's Jutsu to be Interrupted as it falls into a short psychotic break. Once a creature is interrupted in this way, this condition ends.

conditions.name: Psychosis
conditions.description:
Creatures with Psychosis suffer warped perception, hallucinations and emotional instability, suffering the following effects:
  • Disadvantage on Insight and Perception Checks.
  • Disadvantage on Attack Rolls.
  • When casting a Jutsu that requires HS (Hand Signs), they must first make an Illusions Check vs your Genjutsu Save DC. On a failure they increase the cost of the jutsu being cast by +12.
  • This condition ends at the end of an affected creature's turn.

conditions.name: Suppressed
conditions.description:
A Suppressed creature suffers reduced capability as the poison flows through them, suffering the following effects:
  • Cannot Cast Jutsu that cost more than 10 Chakra.
  • Reduce the number of Damage die rolled by 1, starting from the largest damage dice rolled.
  • This condition ends at the end of an affected creature's turn.

conditions.name: Blighted
conditions.description:
A Blighted creature suffers the following effects:
  • A Blighted creature cannot gain Jutsu based bonuses to its AC or Damage.
  • An Envenomed creature cannot take its Bonus Action as it is suffering from severe poisoning.
  • A Corroded creature takes bonus poison damage equal to your Channeled Healing bonus from melee and ranged attacks, once per turn.
  • A Blighted creature can spend their Action retching to expel the toxins in their body, ending this condition.

conditions.name: Hemorrhage Vitality
conditions.description:
Creatures with Hemorrhage Vitality suffer their body's blood vessels and chakra network hemorrhaging, with the following effects while they have this condition:
  • They take 6d6 Necrotic and 6d6 Chakra damage at the beginning of each of their turns.
  • Cannot regain hit points.
  • Physical Conditions except this one cannot be removed.

A creature with this condition makes a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC at the end of each of their turns to end this condition.

conditions.name: Life Pulse
conditions.description:
A creature with Life Pulse has their wounds close, blood replenished when lost, and their sense of pain dulled. They gain the following benefits:
  • They regain 4d6 Hit points and 2d6 Chakra Points at the beginning of each of their turns.
  • They make saving throws and checks to end conditions at advantage.

conditions.name: Cursed
conditions.description:
Curse of the Reaper. A Cursed creature suffers the following Hex effects of the Reaper's choice when marked. This cannot be changed for its duration unless the creature is marked again later.

Vitality Hex: A creature marked with Vitality Hex has their lifeforce siphoned when they take necrotic damage. Once per turn, when a cursed creature takes Necrotic damage, you or an allied creature within 20 feet of you gains temporary hit points equal to your Channeled Healing bonus.

Tributing Hex: A creature marked with Tributing Hex has their Stamina siphoned when they take necrotic damage. When a cursed creature takes Necrotic damage, they also take Chakra damage equal to your Channeled Healing bonus, and once per turn when you deal Chakra damage to a creature, you or an allied creature within 20 feet of you gains temporary chakra points equal to your Channeled Healing bonus.

Withering Hex: A creature marked with Withering Hex has their ability to resist jutsu hindered. The Cursed creature suffers a -1 penalty to saving throws against jutsu and effects that creatures other than yourself would inflict upon them. This penalty increases to -2 at 10th level and -3 at 18th level.

conditions.name: Transfiguration
conditions.description:
A Transfigured creature suffers one of the following effects based on the chosen designation. This cannot be changed for its duration unless the creature is Transfigured again.

Avian: A transfigured (Avian) creature loses bone density and weight. They make saving throws and checks to resist being moved against their will, knocked prone, or Dazed with a -2 penalty.

Carnivoran: A transfigured (Carnivoran) creature loses their ability to defend themselves effectively, suffering a -2 penalty to their Dexterity and Constitution saving throws.

Herbivore: A transfigured (Herbivore) creature loses their ability to fight effectively, suffering a penalty to the damage they deal — reducing the number of dice they roll for damage by 1.

Insectoid: A transfigured (Insectoid) creature loses their ability to avoid encounters. They cannot cast jutsu with a Casting time of Reaction that grants a bonus to their Saving throws or AC.

conditions.name: Tidal Draw
conditions.description: A creature afflicted with Tidal Draw can feel their chakra network being siphoned while kneading chakra. When a Tidal Drawn creature casts a jutsu that requires CM (Chakra Molding), the Ninjutsu Specialist that inflicted this condition gains temporary chakra equal to the rank of the jutsu cast (D-Rank/C-Rank: 1d4, B-Rank/A-Rank: 1d8, S-Rank: 1d12).

conditions.name: Disoriented
conditions.description:
Creatures with Disoriented are dizzy, off guard, and seeing double, triple, or however many of you there are. They suffer the following effects:
  • Disadvantage on Perception Checks.
  • Cannot make attacks of opportunity or trigger reactions that would make an attack.
  • When attempting to target a creature, roll a d20. On a roll of 11 or higher, they target the closest Bunshin instead — even if it is out of the attack, jutsu, or feature's range — causing the attack/jutsu/feature to miss.
  • This condition ends at the end of an affected creature's turn.

conditions.name: Momentary Flaw
conditions.description:
Creatures with Momentary Flaw have a weakness that only the Scout that identified it can exploit. They suffer the following effects:
  • Increase the damage they take from the inflicting Scout-Nin by +2. This increases to +4 at 10th and +6 at 17th level.
  • Increase the chakra cost to cast jutsu with a casting time of 1 Reaction by +6. This increases to +12 at 10th and +18 at 17th level.
  • Jutsu the Scout casts with the Barrier keyword that summons a structure or construct that intercepts damage reduces the amount of hit points it has — or damage it intercepts — by half.

conditions.name: Ghostburn
conditions.description:
Creatures with Ghostburn have had their chakra network overloaded by Ghostfire, suffering the following effects:
  • A Ghostburned creature reduces the damage or healing dice they roll from jutsu they cast by half.
  • A Ghostburned creature that makes a chakra control check to maintain concentration on a jutsu takes 6d6 chakra damage, once per turn.
  • A Ghostburned creature remakes their saving throw to end this condition at the end of each of their turns. They cannot make this saving throw at advantage.

conditions.name: Bone Broken
conditions.description:
Creatures with Bone Broken have had their skeletal structure rattled and injured. They suffer the following effects:
  • Disadvantage on Constitution Saving Throws.
  • Cannot Dash, Disengage, or Dodge.
  • Reduce affected creature's DR to 0.
  • Disarms all weapons or items it is holding and cannot hold weapons or items in their hands.
  • This condition ends at the end of an affected creature's turn.

conditions.name: Balance Broken
conditions.description:
Creatures with Balance Broken have had their balance thrown off, finding it difficult to recover. They suffer the following effects:
  • Disadvantage on Strength Saving Throws.
  • Cannot gain bonuses to AC or DR as a result of Jutsu.
  • If an affected creature would willingly move more than 5 feet or cast a Jutsu with the Mobility (M) component, they must spend their Reaction to maintain balance — otherwise they fall prone, unable to stand until the beginning of their next turn.
  • This condition ends at the end of an affected creature's turn.

conditions.name: Flow Broken
conditions.description:
Creatures with Flow Broken have had their combat flow broken. They have functionally stopped in their tracks as they recover. They suffer the following effects:
  • Disadvantage on Dexterity Saving throws.
  • An affected creature cannot cast jutsu that share keywords with jutsu they've casted since the beginning of their last turn.
  • Affected creature's initiative count is reduced by 4. If this would cause them to shift position in initiative, they move positions at the beginning of the next round.
  • This condition ends at the end of an affected creature's turn.

conditions.name: Shattered
conditions.description:
Your defenses have been sundered and exposed.
  • Attacks targeting a Shattered creature gain a +1 bonus to their critical threat range.
  • A Shattered creature that suffers a critical hit takes bonus damage equal to 2 Martial Die. This increases to 3 Martial Die at 10th and 4 Martial Die at 17th level.
  • A Shattered creature increases damage taken by +2. This increases to +4 if the inflictor is 10th level and +6 at 17th level.
  • The first time you succeed a Strength, Dexterity or Constitution saving throw while you are Shattered, you must remake your saving throw, subtracting the result of 1 Martial Die, taking the lower result. This condition ends immediately.
  • A Shattered creature can spend their action making a DC 20 Strength check to end this condition on a success.

conditions.name: Shaken (Okuri-Inu Fang)
conditions.description:
The inflicting strike left your body with jitters and shakes from its impact. (Class-specific version inflicted by the Weapon Specialist's Okuri-Inu Fang feature.)
  • A Shaken creature can only make one attack on their turn.
  • A Shaken creature that casts a Jutsu on their turn as an Action increases its cost by 1 Flurry Die. This increases to 2 Flurry Die at 10th and 3 Flurry Die at 17th level.
  • This condition ends at the end of an affected creature's turn.

conditions.name: Hobbled
conditions.description:
The inflicting strike disabled your defense and agility, making you a sitting duck.
  • A Hobbled creature cannot move further than 5 feet on their turn.
  • A Hobbled creature makes saving throws to resist being moved at disadvantage.
  • A Hobbled creature makes melee weapon and Taijutsu attacks at disadvantage.
  • This condition ends at the end of an affected creature's turn.

conditions.name: Provoked
conditions.description:
You are provoked, finding it difficult to ignore the provoker, with an instinctual desire to bring harm to them.
  • While Provoked, if you attack or cast a jutsu targeting or affecting a creature other than the one that provoked you, you suffer a -2 penalty to your attack rolls against those creatures. The penalty increases to -3 at 10th and -4 at 17th level.
  • While Provoked, if you attack or cast a jutsu targeting or affecting a creature other than the one that provoked you, you suffer a -1 to your Jutsu save DCs against those creatures. The penalty increases to -2 at 10th and -3 at 17th level.
  • While Provoked, if you attack or cast a jutsu targeting or affecting a creature other than the one that provoked you, the attack made or jutsu cast can also trigger opportunity attacks from the Provoker.

conditions.name: Reaver Echo
conditions.description:
You have been struck, but the damage seems to have been delayed. When it triggers, it detonates like a bomb with deep gashes, slashes, puncture wounds and kinetic impacts that seem to manifest as a punishment.
  • Reaver Echoes detonate if one of the following occurs:
      ◦ You move 10 or more feet on a turn.
      ◦ You make one or more attacks.
      ◦ You would force two or more creatures to make a saving throw as a result of jutsu you cast or feature/trait you use.
  • When Reaver Echoes are detonated, you take 1d12 Piercing or Slashing damage (the Reaver's choice) for every Reaver Echo detonated.
  • When Reaver Echoes are detonated, you lose all generated stacks of them, and they cannot be reapplied again until the end of the current turn.

conditions.name: Shadow Bound
conditions.description:
Shadow Possession – Shadow Bound. [Physical]
  • A Shadow Bound creature is Restrained and Grappled in place.
  • This condition ends early if any of the following occurs:
      ◦ A Shadow Bound creature is moved as a result of another creature or effect moving them.
      ◦ The creature that bound you is incapacitated or cannot mold chakra.
      ◦ The creature that bound you cannot see you.
      ◦ You or the creature that bound you is covered by total darkness.
  • When you would make or remake your saving throw to end this effect, you do not roll at disadvantage.

conditions.name: Infested
conditions.description:
Festering Brood – Infested. [Physical]
Your body has been infiltrated by a small colony of carnivorous insects. Infested is treated as a physical condition.
  • While Infested, increase the cost of jutsu you cast by +4 for each rank of Infested you have.
      ◦ If enhanced, instead, increase the chakra cost by +6 for each rank.
  • While Infested, you gain a -2 penalty to Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
      ◦ If enhanced, the affected creature suffers the same penalty to their jutsu save DCs.
  • An Infested creature can make a Constitution saving throw vs your highest jutsu save DC at the end of each of their turns to remove the infestation on a success.

conditions.name: Network Distortion
conditions.description:
Eight Trigrams – Network Distortion. Max Ranks: 5. [Physical]
Your chakra network has been damaged, making it difficult to control your chakra.
  • While Network Distorted, increase the cost of jutsu cast by +4 for each rank.
  • While Network Distorted, you suffer a -2 penalty to chakra control checks for each rank.
  • You lose 1 rank of Network Distortion at the end of each of your turns.

conditions.name: Well Rested
conditions.description:
Deep Sleep – Well Rested. [Mental / Will Amp]
You ever slept so well; it boosted your capabilities? Well now you have.
  • Fresh Start. You gain temporary chakra points equal to your level. These temporary chakra points last until used or your next rest.
  • Quick Start. The next initiative roll you make is at advantage.
  • Loose Shoulders. The first instance of damage you deal is increased by +1 damage die. If multiple damage die of different sizes are rolled, add one additional die of the largest size.
  • Quick Reaction. The first reaction you take in combat does not consume your reaction for the round. You can use this effect once per application of this status.
  • No Longer Rested. Once all of these benefits have been consumed or used, this status ends.

conditions.name: Coordinated
conditions.description:
Team Coordination – Coordinated. [Mental]
You move as a team, with your team. The variant applied depends on the encounter type — Combat Coordinated for combat, Diplomatic Coordination for social encounters. Both end when the encounter ends.

Combat Coordinated (Will Amp).
  • Coordinated Start. As part of rolling initiative, you gain a rank of Mastery to your initiative. This becomes 2 ranks at 7th and 3 ranks at 15th level.
  • Team Coordination. You can select a willing ally's creature to go in your place in initiative. This counts as their turn for initiative. If you take another Combat Coordinated creature's place in initiative, you must give your place in initiative to another Combat Coordinated creature.
  • Coordinated Support. If another Combat Coordinated creature is under the effect of a condition or hostile effect that requires an ability or skill check, or a saving throw, you can spend your reaction to make that check or saving throw for them using your statistics.

Diplomatic Coordination (Social Amp).
  • Coordinated Words. You gain a rank of Mastery to either Deception, Persuasion or Intimidation (pick one) for the remainder of this social encounter. This becomes 2 ranks at 7th and 3 ranks at 15th level.
  • Coordinated Experience. If your Background is related to or associated with the opposing creature, you gain advantage on Insight checks.

conditions.name: Drained
conditions.description:
Mokuton Infusion – Drained. Max Ranks: 5. [Physical], [Mental]
  • A Drained creature takes 1d8 chakra damage at the start of each of their turns for each rank it has.
  • A Drained creature increases the chakra damage they take from hostile creatures attacks or jutsu by +2. This increases to +4 at 7th level and +6 at 15th level.
  • A Drained creature can spend an Action to make a DC 15 Constitution ability check to end this condition.

conditions.name: Off-Guard
conditions.description:
Advanced Adaptation – Off-Guard. [Physical], [Mental], [Sensory]
  • An Off-Guard creature suffers a -2 penalty to their AC.
  • An Off-Guard creature suffers a -2 penalty to the first Strength or Dexterity saving throw they make per turn.
  • An Off-Guard creature suffers the effects of the Off-Guard bonus from Uchiha clan Hijutsu.

conditions.name: 5-Pronged Seal
conditions.description:
5-Pronged Seal. [Physical], [Mental], [Sensory]
You have the legendary 5-Pronged Seal etched onto you. For the next hour, this seal binds your power significantly, inflicting the following effects.
  • Increase the cost of Jutsu by +10.
  • Reduce the damage you deal by -8.
  • Reduce the number of Jutsu you can concentrate on by -1.
  • A creature can spend its action to make a DC 20 Ninshou check to remove this condition. On a failure, the DC increases by +5.
  • Once you end this condition, you cannot gain it again for 1 Day.

conditions.name: River Dam Seal
conditions.description:
River Dam Seal. [Physical], [Mental], [Sensory]
The ultimate sealing technique. Its power adjusts to the strength of the one bound with it. While a creature has River Dam Seal, the Sealed conditions, effects are suppressed, but they are still present on a creature. A creature can only remove ranks of Sealed one at a time per effect or action that would remove them. While they are afflicted with River Dam Seal, but cannot gain new ranks of Sealed while afflicted with River Dam Seal. A creature with River Dam Seal suffers the following effects based on their Adversary Classification.

Applies to All Afflicted Creatures.
  • Increase the cost of Jutsu by +20.
  • Afflicted creature cannot teleport.
  • Afflicted creature cannot maintain concentration on jutsu.
  • A creature with 0 ranks of Sealed loses this condition.

Applies to Standards.
  • Reduce the damage you deal by -8.
  • Afflicted creature cannot cast jutsu that require Chakra Molding (CM) that are of C-Rank or higher.

Applies to Elites.
  • Reduce the damage you deal by -10.
  • You cannot regain Tenacity die.
  • Afflicted creature cannot cast jutsu that require Chakra Molding (CM) that are of B-Rank or higher.

Applies to Solo's.
  • Reduce the damage you deal by -12.
  • Whenever you spend a Tenacity die, you lose 1 Tenacity die.
  • Afflicted creature cannot cast jutsu that require Chakra Molding (CM) that are of A-Rank or higher.

Applies to Player Characters.
  • Reduce the damage you deal by -10.
  • Afflicted creature cannot upcast.
  • Afflicted creature cannot cast jutsu that require Chakra Molding (CM) that are of their highest rank known.

conditions.name: Mental Overwrite
conditions.description:
Mind Body Transfer – Mental Overwrite. Max Ranks: 5. [Mental], [Sensory]
Pockets of the casters consciousness are overwriting yours. Your thoughts become hazy. Your movements sluggish and you begin to believe, not even your own. The strength of this jutsu depends on the mental strength of those it affects, unless they are Dominated.

Applies to Minions.
  • With 1 rank of Mental Overwrite, the afflicter can choose to make the affected creature becomes Dominated (see effect), until dispelled.

Applies to Standards.
  • With 3 ranks of Mental Overwrite, the afflicter can choose to make the affected creature becomes Dominated for up to 1 hour.
  • This Dominated creature remakes their saves at the end of each of their turns, ending the condition and removing all ranks.

Applies to Elites.
  • With 5 ranks of Mental Overwrite, the afflicter can choose to make the affected creature becomes Dominated for up to 1 minute.
  • This Dominated creature remakes their saves at the end of each of their turns, ending the condition and removing all ranks.

Applies to Solo's.
  • With 5 ranks of Mental Overwrite, the afflicter can choose to make the affected creature becomes Dominated until the end of their next turn, ending the condition and removing all ranks.

Applies to Player Characters.
  • With 5 ranks of Mental Overwrite, the afflicter can choose to make the affected creature becomes Dominated, remaking their Charisma saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending it on a successful save.

conditions.name: Dominated
conditions.description:
Dominated — Extended Condition.
A Dominated creature has their wills overwritten by the inflicting creature, known as the Dominator. The Dominator imposes the following effects on the Dominated and themselves. You can normally be a Dominator to one creature at a time, unless otherwise specified.

Dominator Effects.
  • The Dominators body falls limp, becoming Stunned, Blind, Deafened and Incapacitated, for the duration of this status.
  • The Dominator takes over the body of the Dominated, moving they now act in initiative, when the Dominated would act, speaks in their voice, dialect, languages and even has knowledge of the inflicters mannerisms.
  • The Dominator gains limited access to the Dominated statistics, becoming aware of their General and Clan traits, if any.
  • The Dominator retains access to their known jutsu while in the body of the Dominated.
  • The Dominator must maintain concentration on this status, as if it's an S-Rank Genjutsu or Ninjutsu, that they do not spend chakra to maintain.
  • The Dominator uses their own attack bonuses and save DC when calculating them for the purpose of casting jutsu.
  • The Dominator can use any action, attack or special ability that doesn't cost tenacity die or a special Class or class specific resource, using their own attack bonuses or Save DC for calculation.
  • This status ends, if the Dominators original body takes damage.

Dominated Effects.
  • The Dominated forgoes their turn to the Dominator.
  • The Dominated is aware of all things occurring while under this status.
  • The Dominated's body is even shifting.
  • If this Dominated's body takes damage, they remake their Charisma saving throw, ending this status on a success.
  • A Dominated creature, cannot be Dominated again for 24 hours.
